summary: SisLib decomp progress focused on src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.c with one header prototype correction. The diff cleans several generated-looking shapes, adds or rewrites SisLib text encoding/editing routines, improves typed arguments for HSD_SisLib_803A7684, and records remaining known mismatches as regalloc/scheduling/literal-pooling TODOs. Automated bot output reports score improvements for seven sislib functions, but no human review comments are present.

key_files.role: Primary SisLib decomp source; contains allocator TODO refinements, rewrites of text encoding/emission logic, added HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, and changes to HSD_SisLib_803A7684/HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C/HSD_SisLib_803A8134/HSD_SisLib_803A84BC call shapes.
evidence_refs: diff:sislib.c HSD_SisLib_803A5798 TODO changed to 99.40% with regalloc and literal-pooling notes
diff:sislib.c HSD_SisLib_803A594C TODO changed to 92.57% with coalesce block scheduling/regalloc notes
diff:sislib.c HSD_SisLib_803A67EC rewritten from goto/dead locals/#if0 blocks to for/do loops and u8 pointer LUT walking
diff:sislib.c HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 rewritten with buffer[256], encoded[128], va_list, cursor writes through alloc->data_0
diff:sislib.c HSD_SisLib_803A8134 changes HSD_SisLib_803A7684(text, *cursor, ...) to HSD_SisLib_803A7684(text, cursor, ...)

key_files.role: Header prototype updated to match recovered HSD_SisLib_803A7684 argument types.
evidence_refs: diff:sislib.h changes void HSD_SisLib_803A7684(UNK_T, u8, u8) to void HSD_SisLib_803A7684(HSD_Text*, u8*, u8)

what_changed.text: Updated match-progress TODO comments for HSD_SisLib_803A5798 and HSD_SisLib_803A594C with more specific remaining-diff descriptions, including regalloc/scheduling and literal pooling.
evidence_refs: diff:sislib.c top hunks for HSD_SisLib_803A5798 and HSD_SisLib_803A594C

what_changed.text: Reworked HSD_SisLib_803A67EC from label/goto and dead temporary-heavy code into structured loops that encode ASCII punctuation/digits/letters and search the Shift-JIS lookup table with u8 pointers.
Reworked HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 to use local formatting/encoded buffers, va_list formatting, explicit allocation growth, byte copying, and cursor-style SIS output writes through alloc->data_0.

Added HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, which finds an existing SIS entry, re-encodes formatted text, grows or shrinks the backing buffer, shifts tail bytes, writes replacement encoded bytes, and returns a success flag.
Recovered HSD_SisLib_803A7684 as taking HSD_Text*, u8* cursor, and u8 flags, and updated call sites plus the public header prototype accordingly.
Automated report shows score improvements for HSD_SisLib_803A7684, HSD_SisLib_803A84BC, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, HSD_SisLib_803A67EC, HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C, and HSD_SisLib_803A8134.

smart_moves.text: Kept unknown global/function address-style names where semantics are not proven while improving local variable roles such as old_buf, new_buf, cursor, encoded, buffer, playhead, tail_src, and bulk_count.
Updated the owning header when a function body/call-site evidence changed HSD_SisLib_803A7684 from UNK_T/u8 to HSD_Text*/u8*.
Documented known remaining mismatch causes in TODO comments rather than hiding them as completed matches.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When generated-looking code contains many dead_rXX/dead_spXX locals, labels, and disabled #if 0 blocks, try rebuilding it as structured source with explicit local buffers, role-named cursors, and natural loops before preserving the decompiler shape.
evidence_refs: diff:sislib.c HSD_SisLib_803A67EC removes dead_rXX locals, labels loop_42/loop_37, and #if 0 blocks
diff:sislib.c HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 removes many dead_sp/dead_r locals and uses buffer[256]/encoded[128]

decomp_lessons.lesson: If body and call-site evidence recover a pointer argument, update both the implementation and the owning header rather than leaving an UNK_T or scalar placeholder.
evidence_refs: diff:sislib.c HSD_SisLib_803A7684 call sites pass cursor
diff:sislib.h HSD_SisLib_803A7684 prototype changes to HSD_Text*, u8*, u8

decomp_lessons.lesson: Remaining regalloc, scheduling, and literal-pooling mismatches should be called out narrowly in TODOs when the PR evidence does not support claiming a full match.

naming_conventions.text: Address-style SisLib symbols such as HSD_SisLib_803A67EC, HSD_SisLib_803A6B98, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, and lbl_8040C8C0 remain in use where no semantic global/function names are established.
evidence_refs: diff:sislib.c function names and lbl_8040C8C0 references

naming_conventions.text: Local variables move away from temp/dead register names toward role names: old_buf/new_buf for allocation movement, encoded/buffer for text conversion, cursor/playhead for SIS stream position, and bulk_count/tail_count for copy loops.
Recovered signature uses HSD_Text* for the text object and u8* cursor for SIS byte stream traversal in HSD_SisLib_803A7684.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Used natural for/do-while loops to replace label/goto traversal in HSD_SisLib_803A67EC, including bounded input traversal up to 0x80 and a do-while LUT scan over 0x11F entries.
Used explicit byte-copy loops with 8-byte bulk chunks followed by tail-copy loops in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 and HSD_SisLib_803A70A0.
Used cursor writes through a pointer to alloc->data_0 in HSD_SisLib_803A6B98: cur = (u8**) &alloc->data_0; *(*cur)++ = ...
Adjusted PAD_STACK in HSD_SisLib_803A8134 from 16 to 0x8; PR evidence shows the edit but does not include an explanation or verifier output for why that exact padding is required.

TODO comments identify specific unresolved codegen differences: HSD_SisLib_804D7970 pointer register allocation/literal pooling in HSD_SisLib_803A5798 and coalesce-block add/register reuse in HSD_SisLib_803A594C.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Bot report lists seven unmatched-item score improvements in main/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib, led by HSD_SisLib_803A7684 from 0.00% to 55.05%, HSD_SisLib_803A84BC from 66.31% to 89.15%, and HSD_SisLib_803A70A0 from 0.00% to 78.79%. No human review comments are present in the loaded slice.

evidence_refs: metadata_json for PR identity, merge state, author, changed-file counts
raw diff excerpt and raw/diff.diff for source edits
notes: No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded slice.
Diff evidence is strong for what changed, but the loaded raw diff is marked truncated in the packet; targeted lookup was used only for the header hunk and function hunk classification.
Automated report gives fuzzy percentage improvements, not proof that functions fully match.
TODO comments mention remaining mismatch causes, but the slice does not include local build, objdiff, or regression logs.

accepted_candidate_records.title: SisLib PR 2540: generated dead-local cleanup via structured loops and role variables
accepted_candidate_records.text: In sislib.c, PR 2540 replaced dead_rXX/dead_spXX-heavy generated shapes and labels in HSD_SisLib_803A67EC/HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 with structured loops, local buffers, and role-named cursors. This supports a reusable decomp lesson to try authored-looking loop/cursor forms before keeping generated-looking dead temporaries.

accepted_candidate_records.title: SisLib PR 2540: recovered pointer signatures should reach the owning header
accepted_candidate_records.text: HSD_SisLib_803A7684 call sites and implementation use an HSD_Text* and u8* cursor, and sislib.h was updated from UNK_T/u8/u8 to HSD_Text*/u8*/u8. This is a graph-safe example of synchronizing recovered function signatures with headers.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR 2540 as an example under natural-loops and conservative-naming
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2540 provides concrete examples of converting generated labels/dead temporaries into structured loops and role-named locals while retaining address-style symbols where semantics remain unknown.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards already cover the practice; curator may want to add this PR as an evidence/example record rather than a new rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR 2540 as an example for truthful header updates after signature recovery
source_update_candidates.text: HSD_SisLib_803A7684 changed from UNK_T/u8/u8 to HSD_Text*/u8*/u8 in sislib.h after implementation/call-site recovery.
source_update_candidates.reason: Good historical example of replacing unknown header types with recovered concrete types.

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate whether sislib cursor/buffer structs deserve owned types
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2540 repeatedly casts allocation fields and byte streams, including cur = (u8**) &alloc->data_0 and ((sisLib_803A7664_t*) alloc)->xC++. This may indicate recoverable internal SisLib buffer/cursor structure, but the PR slice alone does not prove a final type layout.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential future type recovery; should not be promoted as a path fact without broader source evidence.

rejection_notes: Do not record the bot percentage improvements as full matches; they are reported as unmatched-item score improvements only.
Do not infer human reviewer intent or acceptance rationale; no human review comments are present.
Do not promote PAD_STACK(0x8) as a general tactic from this slice alone; the diff shows the edit but not verifier evidence.
Do not rename address-style HSD_SisLib symbols based only on this PR; the diff preserves address-style names.
